title: In /Users/masayuki/Dev/cursorvers/claude-config, verify test coverage/evidence for the Phase 0 handoff pipeline change. Compare against the 7-item test plan and acceptance criteria in ~/.claude/cache/handoff-phase0-codex.md and ~/.claude/cache/dev-spec-phase0-handoff-pipeline.md. Return missing test...
summary_text: **Findings** 1. `codex-kernel-guard launch` now does execute a real preflight fan-out (GLM + specialist) through scheduler/quorum, but Codex evidence is still synthetic: quorum only checks prompt generation (`run_codex_prompt_check`) and the Codex success increment comes from run-driver completion, not a Codex lane execution ([scripts/lib/kernel-launch-quorum.sh:68](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-launch-quorum.sh:68), [scripts/lib/kernel-launch-quorum.sh:91](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-launch-quorum.sh:91), [scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-run-driver.sh:317](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-run-driver.sh:317)). 2. Launch preflight can fail with a misleading timeout when scheduler dispatches nothing (e.g., project concurrency defers launch): guard assumes `.launched[0]` exists and times out instead of handling deferred/not-launched states explicitly ([scripts/codex-kernel-guard.sh:634](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/codex-kernel-guard.sh:634), [scripts/codex-kernel-guard.sh:637](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/codex-kernel-guard.sh:637), [scripts/kernel-scheduler.sh:384](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/kernel-scheduler.sh:384)). 3. Timeout path does not terminate the spawned run-driver, so preflight failures can leave background execution/claims alive and contaminate subsequent launches ([scripts/codex-kernel-guard.sh:497](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/codex-kernel-guard.sh:497), [scripts/codex-kernel-guard.sh:637](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/codex-kernel-guard.sh:637), [scripts/kernel-scheduler.sh:314](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/kernel-scheduler.sh:314)). 4. Simulation test remains synthetic (manual `record-provider`), so it does not validate end-to-end launch orchestration evidence; only the launch-focused test does ([tests/test-kernel-working-orchestration-sim.sh:31](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/tests/test-kernel-working-orchestration-sim.sh:31), [tests/test-kernel-working-orchestration-sim.sh:32](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/tests/test-kernel-working-orchestration-sim.sh:32), [tests/test-kernel-working-orchestration-sim.sh:33](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/tests/test-kernel-working-orchestration-sim.sh:33)). **Residual risks** - Provider evidence can still be spoofed by direct ledger writes because health checks trust counters, not provenance/source class ([scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-health.sh:187](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-health.sh:187), [scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-ledger.sh:220](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-ledger.sh:220)). - Contract says non-interactive launch must preflight; current non-interactive path does, but `ORCH_DRY_RUN=1` bypasses preflight entirely ([scripts/codex-kernel-guard.sh:629](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/codex-kernel-guard.sh:629), [docs/kernel/kernel-working-orchestration-contract-v1.md:54](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/docs/kernel/kernel-working-orchestration-contract-v1.md:54)). Executed tests: `tests/test-kernel-launch-working-orchestration.sh` passed; `tests/test-kernel-working-orchestration-sim.sh` passed.
